headline.convention: full Dirac operator, both chiralities, both eigenvalue signs (complex spinor bundle of dim 4)
headline.comparison_to_scalar_KG3: Both spatial Casimir energies are pure rationals with no sqrt extensions: scalar 1/240 vs Dirac 17/480.  The Dirac case lives in Q because the Hurwitz shift is rational (3/2) and Bernoulli polynomials are Q-coefficient.
headline.pi_first_enters_through: temporal compactification with anti-periodic boundary conditions; lowest non-zero mode is omega_0^t = pi / beta (NB: not 2 pi / beta as in the bosonic case)
headline.verdict_overall: Spatial Dirac Casimir on S^3 is pure-rational (one-projection: spatial mode-counting only).  Adding S^1_beta with AP fermion boundary conditions is a second projection that introduces pi at the lowest temporal mode.  Same mechanism as KG-3 scalar; spinor sector is in the same algebraic-extension ring as the scalar sector.

spatial_T0_Dirac_Casimir.convention_computed: FULL DIRAC: both chiralities, both eigenvalue signs, complex spinor bundle of dim 4 on S^3

transcendental_ledger.transcendentals: none -- |lambda_n| in Q (half-integer); g_n in Z+

transcendental_ledger.value: = 4 [zeta_R(s - 2, 3/2) - (1/4) zeta_R(s, 3/2)] (Hurwitz form via shift m = n + 3/2)
transcendental_ledger.transcendentals: none in the symbolic form; Hurwitz zeta has rational values at negative integers when the shift parameter is rational

transcendental_ledger.step: (4) Hurwitz zeta values via zeta_R(-n, a) = -B_{n+1}(a)/(n+1)

textbook_comparison.textbook_reference_primary: Camporesi & Higuchi, J. Geom. Phys. 20 (1996) 1, Eq. 5.27 (Dirac spectrum on S^d, d=3 case).
textbook_comparison.textbook_reference_secondary: Bytsenko, Cognola, Elizalde, Moretti, Zerbini, 'Analytic Aspects of Quantum Fields', World Scientific 2003, sec. 4.6 (spinor Casimir on spheres).
textbook_comparison.textbook_reference_tertiary: Kennedy, Critchley, Dowker, Ann. Phys. (NY) 125 (1980) 346 (early systematic treatment).

temperature_correction_sketch.AP_temporal_mode_formula: omega_k^t = (2k + 1) pi / beta, k in Z
temperature_correction_sketch.P_bosonic_temporal_mode_formula_for_comp: omega_k^t = 2 pi k / beta, k in Z (periodic bosons; KG-2)
temperature_correction_sketch.smallest_nonzero_AP_mode_at_beta_eq_1: pi (from k = 0 OR k = -1; AP has NO zero mode)
temperature_correction_sketch.pi_injection_step: appears in the temporal eigenvalue equation as the factor (2k+1) pi / beta -- the same circumference-to-radian ratio that injects pi in the bosonic case (KG-2), but shifted by 1/2 in the index due to AP boundary conditions
temperature_correction_sketch.key_qualitative_difference_from_bosonic: Bosons (periodic) have a k = 0 ZERO mode (omega = 0); fermions (AP) have NO zero mode -- the lowest temporal mode is already omega = pi / beta.  This means the fermion thermal sum has no infrared zero-mode divergence to worry about, but the lowest MODE itself carries pi.  The pi-injection happens at the lowest step of the temporal sum, not at any later analytic-continuation step.
temperature_correction_sketch.full_thermal_Dirac_Casimir_calculation_n: The full thermal Dirac Casimir on S^3 x S^1_beta is the Epstein-Hurwitz double sum over (n, k) with the AP shift and the Camporesi-Higuchi spatial spectrum.  This is a standard but technically involved zeta-regularized calculation (see Bytsenko et al. sec. 4.6 for the analytic structure).  We do NOT reproduce it here -- the pi-injection mechanism at the spectrum level is the verification scope of this sprint.
